description:
You are acting as a configuration-manager who must take a **point-in-time backup** of the project configuration that lives in

    /home/user/project

and then prove that the backup is bit-for-bit identical once it is restored.

The directory already exists and currently contains the exact files shown below:

1. /home/user/project/app.conf  
2. /home/user/project/db.conf  
3. /home/user/project/env/dev.env  
4. /home/user/project/env/prod.env  

(The contents of those files are guaranteed to be present before you start; you do **not** have to create or modify them.)

Your assignment, carried out only from the Linux terminal, is:

A. Create a compressed archive of /home/user/project named  
   /home/user/config_backup_20230101.tar.gz

B. Create a new empty directory  

       /home/user/restore  

   and extract the archive there so that, after extraction, the path  

       /home/user/restore/project  

   has the same four files and the same directory structure.

C. Produce a verification log file named  

       /home/user/backup_checksums.log  

   whose contents must follow **exactly** the 5-line format defined below.  
   The automated checker will reject even a single extra space or line-break, so follow the template very closely.

   • Lines 1-4 list the files **in alphabetical order by their path relative to the project root**, each line containing three comma-separated fields:

        <relative_path>,<sha256_original>,<sha256_extracted>

     where  
     – <relative_path> is one of  
          app.conf  
          db.conf  
          env/dev.env  
          env/prod.env  
     – <sha256_original> is the SHA-256 digest of the file that still resides in /home/user/project  
     – <sha256_extracted> is the SHA-256 digest of the counterpart that now resides in /home/user/restore/project  

   • Line 5 is the summary line and must read exactly:  

        SUMMARY,VERIFIED

     (all upper-case, no spaces).

   As an example of the required **shape** (do **NOT** copy these hashes, they will be different on your system), the finished log file must look like:

        app.conf,aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa,aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
        db.conf,bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb,bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb
        env/dev.env,cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc,cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc
        env/prod.env,dddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddd,dddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddd
        SUMMARY,VERIFIED

   Important notes for the log:
   • The four data lines MUST be sorted alphabetically exactly as above.  
   • The two digests on each data line must be identical and must be valid 64-character lower-case hexadecimal strings.  
   • There must be exactly 5 newline-terminated lines (no blank sixth line).

D. When all of the above is complete, the system must therefore contain:

   • /home/user/config_backup_20230101.tar.gz  
   • /home/user/restore/project with an exact copy of the original four files  
   • /home/user/backup_checksums.log formatted exactly as specified.

You do **not** need root privileges, and no network access is required. Producing short, informative console output while you work is encouraged.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent starts):

Directory tree:
/home/user/project
├── app.conf     (contents: "version=1.0\n")
├── db.conf      (contents: "host=localhost\nport=5432\n")
└── env
    ├── dev.env  (contents: "DEBUG=true\n")
    └── prod.env (contents: "DEBUG=false\n")

The automated checker will:
1. Verify that /home/user/config_backup_20230101.tar.gz exists and contains exactly the four files listed above with the same byte-for-byte contents.
2. Verify that /home/user/restore/project exists and is an exact byte-for-byte replica of /home/user/project.
3. Independently compute SHA-256 hashes of each original file and its restored counterpart, then parse /home/user/backup_checksums.log, expecting:

   • The first four lines in this exact order:
        app.conf
        db.conf
        env/dev.env
        env/prod.env

   • Each of those lines must contain three comma-separated fields.
   • Field 2 and Field 3 for a given line must be identical 64-character lowercase hexadecimal strings and must match the checker’s own calculated hash for that file.
   • A fifth and final line equal to:  "SUMMARY,VERIFIED"

4. Confirm that the log file contains exactly 5 newline-terminated lines and nothing else.
name: task_000000_7603e472
